title: libxmljs parseXml with noent: true — external entities are expanded, enabling XXE

params.passDetail: parseXml leaves entity substitution off (noent is not true) — external/general entities are not expanded, so XXE cannot read files or reach internal hosts.
params.failDetail: parseXml is called with noent: true, which turns ON entity substitution. A document with a DOCTYPE that declares an external entity (e.g. SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" or an http:// URL) will have it EXPANDED during parsing — a classic XML External Entity (XXE) attack that leaks local files and enables SSRF against internal services. Remove noent: true; never enable entity substitution on untrusted XML.
params.absentCallDetail: No parseXml call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: parseXml does not set noent; the safe default (no entity substitution) applies.

provenance.note: noent: true is enabled to make a legitimate internal entity resolve, but on any untrusted XML input it opens XXE — one of the highest-impact parser footguns. Nothing errors on well-formed input, so it ships.
